title: agrostis bettyae
intro: Agrostis bettyae, also known as Betty's hair grass, is a species of grass native to the mountainous regions of New Zealand. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, with traditional Maori practitioners using the plant for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Agrostis bettyae include its ability to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. Agrostis bettyae is typically prepared as an infusion or tincture, often in combination with other herbs, to harness its medicinal potential.
